6 
(2005) 12 SCC 77
352 
Supreme Court Reports
52. Execution of a power of attorney in terms of the 
provisions of the Contract Act as also the Powers of 
Attorney Act is valid. A power of attorney, we have noticed 
hereinbefore, is executed by the donor so as to enable 
the donee to act on his behalf. Except in cases where 
power of attorney is coupled with interest, it is revocable. 
The donee in exercise of his power under such power of 
attorney only acts in place of the donor subject of course 
to the powers granted to him by reason thereof. He cannot 
use the power of attorney for his own benefit. He acts in 
a fiduciary capacity. Any act of infidelity or breach of trust 
is a matter between the donor and the donee.”
21. Further, the position of a power of attorney with respect to conferment 
of title was explained by this Court in the case of Suraj Lamp 
(supra), thus:
“20. A power of attorney is not an instrument of transfer
